NY session. All runs had n>=100, but every symbol/timeframe failed WR/PF threshold, so market status is rejected.

Research evidence is index futures only; fx is an untested extrapolation. See docs/research/scalping-20260717/family_orb.md. Validated variant is the 1-minute OR window; on >1m charts the OR degrades to the chart-bar range (unvalidated).

futures.notes: Primary research target (IEEE Access TORB, Grade A source / B in practice). DT TP1/SL overlay is untested vs the EOD-only source exit. Validated variant is the 1-minute OR window; on >1m charts the OR degrades to the chart-bar range (unvalidated). 2026-07-17 setup-verify run: ES1!/NQ1!/YM1! all resolved to *_DL (delayed-data) feed variants — this account has no real-time futures license. 1-minute chart history is hard-capped at ~300-301 bars regardless of scroll/visible-range attempts, and that entire window (2026-07-17T~03:27-08:28Z) falls outside the RTH session (13:30-20:00Z), so 0 trades on all 3 symbols is a data-availability artifact, not a strategy result. No parameter retuning attempted. Re-run once a data plan/account with deeper 1-minute futures history is available.

stocks_us.notes: Validated variant is the 1-minute OR window; on >1m charts the OR degrades to the chart-bar range (unvalidated).

stocks_jp.notes: Validated variant is the 1-minute OR window; on >1m charts the OR degrades to the chart-bar range (unvalidated).

fx.notes: NY preset, declared 10% equity sizing. 5m history ~3.2 months -> n<100 on all four pairs (62-85 trades). All four 15m runs sufficient (n=145-180) and all fail: WR 26-38%, PF 0.19-0.43. Majority (4/4) fail -> rejected. Consistent with registry prior: currency variant weakest in source research.

futures.notes: RTH preset on delayed _DL feeds (ES1!/NQ1!/YM1! resolve to CME_MINI_DL/CBOT_MINI_DL; no realtime futures license). 5m history ~3.4 months -> n<100 (45-49 trades, insufficient_data). All three 15m runs sufficient (n=150-151) and all fail: PF 0.40-0.65 far below 1.3 threshold. Sizing overridden once (recorded): fixed 1 contract instead of 10% equity because contract notional > allocation made every entry size to 0. Majority (3/3 sufficient symbols) fail -> rejected.

stocks_us.notes: SPY variant has a documented negative independent replication; treat as decayed until local backtest proves otherwise.

fx.notes: Source evidence is SPY/ES/NQ; fx untested.

futures.notes: Grade B (SSRN + 3 independent write-ups, no independent OOS). Entries gated to HH:00/HH:30 checkpoints per source; see docs/research/scalping-20260717/family_orderflow.md.

stocks_us.notes: RTH preset, default 10% equity sizing (non-zero trades on every symbol, no override needed). Registry set NASDAQ:AAPL/NVDA/TSLA all resolved to BATS:* (alternate/delayed feed variants). 1m runs (all 3 symbols): n=66-85, n<100 -> insufficient_data; WR/PF not used to decide the label (TSLA 1m numerically WR=36.36%/PF=2.23, which would pass wr>=35&pf>=1.6, but the n-gate is not met so it stays insufficient_data and is excluded from the majority vote, per Codex adversarial review). 5m runs (all 3 symbols, n=379-402, sufficient): AAPL WR=21.37%/PF=0.870, NVDA WR=23.63%/PF=0.886, TSLA WR=24.80%/PF=1.136 -- all fail every WR/PF threshold combination despite TSLA 5m having positive net profit (+0.65%, low-WR/high-payoff skew, not a qualifying edge per Codex review). 3/3 n-sufficient registry symbols fail on 5m -> majority fail -> stocks_us rejected. Market verdict is effectively decided by the 5m timeframe only; the 1m timeframe remains unresolved (insufficient_data), not itself rejected. bt_winrate/bt_pf are the median of the three sufficient 5m runs. NASDAQ:QQQ was run as an informational supplementary symbol (source research family_orderflow.md cites SPY/QQQ-class indices) but is not part of the registry stocks_us symbol set and was excluded from the majority vote; its results (1m insufficient_data n=85, 5m fail WR=24.05%/PF=0.931) are directionally consistent with the registry set. No parameter retuning attempted. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.6-sol) confirmed all insufficient_data/fail labels are internally consistent with the stated criteria and found no outlier/equity distortion in the TSLA 5m result; it flagged that AAPL/NVDA/TSLA are correlated large-cap growth names so the 3-symbol majority is a governance rule, not a claim of independent statistical replication, and recommended the n>=100 gate be read per symbol-and-timeframe run rather than summed across timeframes (already how it was applied here).

fx.notes: Untested extrapolation; source is QQQ only.

stocks_us.notes: RTH preset, default 10% equity sizing, ~1y of 5m data (2025-07-07..2026-07-17). Requested NASDAQ:AAPL/NVDA/TSLA all resolved to BATS:* feed variants (recorded in evidence JSONs), so this is a rejection on BATS-feed data. All 3 registry symbols sufficient (n=234/190/184) and all 3 FAIL: AAPL WR=42.74%/PF=0.891, NVDA WR=46.32%/PF=0.842, TSLA WR=42.39%/PF=0.980 -- NVDA clears the 45% WR branch but PF is far below 1.3, and no symbol reaches PF 1.6 for the 35% branch. maxDD 0.72-1.17%, well under the 15% cap. 3/3 fail -> majority fail -> rejected. bt_winrate/bt_pf are medians of the 3 sufficient registry runs. IMPORTANT QQQ-vs-source note: NASDAQ:QQQ (resolved BATS:QQQ) was run as an informational supplementary symbol because the setup's entire research evidence base is a single 2024-2025 QQQ study claiming strong morning-window (0945-1130) performance; the strategy default entry window resolves exactly to 0945-1130, so this was a same-window test. Result: n=212, WR=49.53%, PF=0.998, essentially breakeven (net -$31 on $1M) -- a failure to replicate the source's claimed PF 1.5+ edge; since the test period (2025-07 onward) postdates the source study window, this reads as an out-of-sample decay/overfit signal against the source study itself, materially weakening the Grade B evidence for every other market too. QQQ is excluded from the 3-symbol majority vote. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.6-sol) confirmed all verdict arithmetic; flagged that (a) 3/3 fail is a governance vote over correlated large-cap tech names, not 3 independent replications, (b) declared strategy costs are commission_type=percent commission_value=0.005 = 0.005% (0.5bp) + slippage 2 ticks -- very low friction, which makes the breakeven QQQ result a stronger negative, (c) shorts are consistently the weaker side on all 4 symbols (long-only PFs 1.05/0.88/1.21/1.01 still all below 1.3, so no post-hoc long-only rescue), (d) TSLA canonical n=184 (winning+losing) vs API top-line 185 is immaterial to the n>=100 gate. No parameter retuning attempted. Prior Grade B research note preserved: single 2024-2025 QQQ study recurring across 5 research passes, morning window strongest, see docs/research/scalping-20260717/family_vwap.md.

fx.notes: Ablation test result: RTH preset used (functionally identical to NY in this Pine script: both resolve to session string 0930-1600 America/New_York). Disaster stop OFF. Fixed-quantity=1-unit sizing kept per instruction (not switched to percent_of_equity for FX), which makes max_dd_pct/net_profit_pct round to ~0.00% on all four pairs -- non-informative for the maxDD<=15% criterion (passes trivially by construction, not by low risk); WR and PF are per-trade return ratios unaffected by this sizing choice and remain the valid basis for the verdict. 5m n<100 on all four pairs -> insufficient_data. 15m sufficient on all four (n=133-150), all fail on WR/PF alone regardless of the maxDD caveat: USDJPY WR28.67%/PF0.364, EURUSD WR32.09%/PF0.570, GBPUSD WR29.32%/PF0.362, AUDUSD WR25.19%/PF0.241. Majority (4/4) fail -> rejected. Ablation vs intraday_momo (overlay, rejected, percent_of_equity 10% sizing, matched-market but NOT matched-n: overlay n=180/160/145/151 vs pure n=150/134/133/135, so this is a descriptive not paired comparison): PF delta mixed (USDJPY -0.003, EURUSD +0.144, GBPUSD +0.002, AUDUSD +0.053) but WR delta worse on all four (-7.44/-5.41/-3.78/-1.30pp). Net: PF stayed in the same clearly-failing range (0.19-0.57) with or without the overlay -- no adoptable edge surfaces either way. Ablation hypothesis NOT supported on FX (inconclusive-to-negative rather than clearly refuted, given the sizing/trade-count mismatch versus the overlay baseline). Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.6-sol, 2026-07-18) confirmed verdict consistency (WR/PF alone fails all 4 symbols regardless of the maxDD sizing caveat), flagged the FX overlay-vs-pure n mismatch as weakening the ablation comparison to descriptive-only, and flagged commission/slippage double-counting risk (0.005% + 2 ticks per fill, not clearly disclosed as round-trip) as a secondary caveat that does not change the fail verdict since all PF stayed well under 1.0.

futures.notes: Ablation test result: RTH preset, disaster stop OFF, Fixed-quantity=1-contract sizing (kept from intraday_momo futures zero-trade-trap fix). Delayed CME_MINI_DL/CBOT_MINI_DL feeds (no realtime license). 5m n<100 on all three symbols -> insufficient_data. 15m sufficient on all three (n=150-151), all fail: ES1! WR31.79%/PF0.356/DD3.03%, NQ1! WR38.00%/PF0.514/DD4.26%, YM1! WR29.33%/PF0.316/DD2.10%. Majority (3/3) fail -> rejected. Ablation vs intraday_momo (overlay, rejected, PF0.531/0.653/0.399 WR36.42/42.0/32.0% on the same symbols/tf, matched n): pure variant is WORSE on every symbol (PF delta -0.175/-0.139/-0.083, WR delta -4.63/-4.00/-2.67pp, DD delta +1.04/+1.49/+0.34pp). Ablation hypothesis REFUTED for futures: removing the SL/TP overlay did not recover the published edge and made results worse; the overlay was mitigating losses, not causing them. Codex adversarial review (gpt-5.6-sol, 2026-07-18) confirmed verdict consistency, flagged cross-symbol non-independence (ES/NQ/YM share US-session shocks -> effective n<3 for the majority vote, though moot since 0/3 pass) and that only a 20-trade (of 151) sample was manually inspected for outlier/tail dependence -- platform maxDD is computed from the full equity curve so the maxDD figures themselves remain valid, but full-sample tail-risk (max single loss, PF excluding top-N trades) was not independently verified.

stocks_us.notes: SPY variant has a documented negative independent replication; low priority.
